notes.anchor: the envoys had not yet returned, whom the Senate sent not to sue for peace but to declare war if their message were not obeyed
notes.note: The first embassy to Antony at Mutina (Jan.–43 BC), carrying the Senate's ultimatum; its failure to make Antony yield led to the war Cicero had urged in the Philippics.

notes.anchor: professed myself the leading man for the Senate and the Roman people
notes.note: Cicero claims the role of princeps, taking the lead in the senatorial resistance to Antony in early 43 BC, the cause of libertas to which his Philippics were devoted.
